title: Plugin chain P3: runtime dependency packaging (ajv/ajv-formats/gray-matter resolution at the user's machine)
description:
Third ticket of the plugin packaging chain (epic TASK-020). Per `tasks/TASK-020.research.md` §B.4: `src/task-store.js` imports `ajv` + `ajv-formats` at module load (and gray-matter patterns are used elsewhere) — but these are currently in `devDependencies`, and an installed plugin's dir is copied to the cache **without** running `npm install`, so `node_modules` won't exist next to the cached `src/`. The init path runs that code on the USER's machine, so the deps must resolve there.

Scope:
1. Move `ajv`, `ajv-formats`, `gray-matter` from `devDependencies` to `dependencies`.
2. Implement the documented `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` first-run install pattern (§B.4 option 2): a `SessionStart` hook diffs the bundled `package.json` against a copy in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}`, runs `npm install` there when they differ, and wires `NODE_PATH=${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/node_modules` for the `bin/` + MCP subprocesses. Survives plugin updates; no repo bloat.
3. Vendoring (esbuild single-file bundle) is the documented alternative if first-run latency is unacceptable — note the trade-off and the fallback path.
acceptance_criteria: `ajv`, `ajv-formats`, and `gray-matter` are in `dependencies` (not `devDependencies`); `package.json` remains valid and the existing test suite still passes.

A mechanism resolves these runtime deps on a user machine WITHOUT a manual `npm install` step — either the ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} first-run install (with NODE_PATH wiring) or a vendored bundle. The chosen mechanism is implemented and documented.

A test or reproducible simulation proves `src/task-store.js` (which imports ajv at load) imports successfully when run with NODE_PATH pointing at the data-dir node_modules (i.e. with no node_modules adjacent to src/).
First-run latency of the chosen mechanism is measured/estimated and recorded; if it triggers the vendoring fallback, that decision is documented.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Minted from TASK-020 spike §F (P3) + §B.4. This is the packaging bug the audit surfaced (AC15 of the §B.2 table). Gates both P4 (init runs task-store on the user machine) and P6 (MCP server imports task-store).
